# Setting Up a Reproducible Analytical System {#lesson01}

:::cdi-message
- **ID:** DS-L01
- **Type:** Premium Foundation
- **Audience:** Intermediate to Advanced
- **Theme:** Reproducibility and structure enable reliable systems
:::

In the foundations track, you learned how to run analysis.

In this track, you will build workflows that must remain:

- reproducible  
- structured  
- reliable over time  

This requires a more deliberate setup.

An environment is not just where code runs.

It is the **foundation of the system you are building**.

---

## What This Setup Supports

By the end of this lesson, you will have a working environment that supports:

- consistent execution across sessions  
- controlled dependencies  
- structured project organization  
- reproducible builds  
- preparation for modeling, pipelines, and deployment  

---

## Project Structure (Important)

## Project Structure (Important)

```text
project-root/
│
├── data/
├── scripts/
│   ├── bash/
│   └── python/
├── models/
├── docs/
├── requirements.txt
├── _quarto.yml
│
├── 00-preface.qmd
├── 01-setting-up-environment.qmd
├── 08-feature-engineering.qmd
...

---

## Python Environment

Activate:

```{bash}
source .venv/bin/activate
```

Verify:

```{bash}
which python
python --version
```

---

## Dependency Control

```{bash}
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

---

## Build the Project

```{bash}
bash scripts/bash/build.sh
```

---

## Sanity Check

Open:

```{bash}
open docs/index.html
```

---

## CDI Insight

Reliable systems depend on stable foundations.

---

## Summary

- `.venv` is active  
- dependencies are controlled  
- build pipeline works  

---

## Next Step

→ Feature engineering and data representation
